title: Schradera elmeri
taxonomy: Schradera elmeri is classified within the family Rubiaceae, which is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The genus Schradera is a part of this family and comprises various species of flowering plants. The classification of Schradera elmeri places it as a species within this genus. The species is native to Southeast Asia, specifically in countries such as the Philippines and Malaysia. Within the classification, Schradera elmeri is further divided into its specific taxonomic hierarchy, including kingdom, phylum, class, order, family, genus, and species.
uses: Schradera elmeri has been used in traditional medicine for its potential to treat various health conditions. intro: Schradera elmeri, also known as Elmer's Schradera or Elmer's Schradera root, is a plant species in the Rubiaceae family, traditionally used as a medicinal plant in the Philippines. The root of Schradera elmeri has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate pain, reduce fever, and combat infections. Bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Schradera elmeri is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make herbal teas and tonics to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections.
